name: pide
ide_drive: single -drive if=ide,format=raw,index=0,file=pide.img (primary master, unit 0); generated by gen_disk.py as the single source of truth for BOTH the qemu backing image (pide.img) and the RTL backing store (pide.disk.hex via $readmemh)
transitions: real->protected (CR0.PE 0->1, CS far-jump to 32-bit flat code segment 0x08)
set nIEN (0x3F6 bit1) -> polled mode, NO IRQ14 ever delivered (fully synchronous, per-record single-step differentiable)

select primary master (0x1F6=0xA0); read RESET SIGNATURE: status(0x1F7)=0x50, error(0x1F1)=0x00, nsector(0x1F2)=1, sector(0x1F3)=1, lcyl(0x1F4)=0, hcyl(0x1F5)=0, devhead(0x1F6)=0xA0, alt-status(0x3F6)=0x50
absent slave: select unit1 (0x1F6=0xB0); status(0x1F7)/error(0x1F1)/alt-status(0x3F6) read 0x00 (qemu masks ONLY these three for the selected absent slave); reselect master

IDENTIFY DEVICE (0xEC): poll BSY=0/DRQ=1 (status 0x58), drain 256 words via inw (each graded per-record): geometry w0=0x0040/w1=cyls=2/w3=heads=16/w6=secs=63, capacity/feature words, config-pinned model 'QEMU HARDDISK'/serial 'QM00001'/firmware '2.5+'; status after drain = 0x50
READ SECTORS (0x20) LBA 0: nsector=1, LBA28 devhead 0xE0; drain 256 words byte-identical to the single-source disk (word 255 = 0xAA55 boot sig); status 0x50
READ SECTORS (0x20) LBA 127 (non-adjacent): proves LBA->offset addressing (sector-127 word 0 = 0x7F00); status 0x50

READ SECTORS (0x20) nsector=2 @ LBA 0 (MULTI-SECTOR): drains 512 words across the sector-0->sector-1 boundary, GATE-PROVING the multi-sector PIO continuation (data_idx==255 && nsec_left>1 -> xfer_lba+1, reload DRQ) matches qemu (req_nb_sectors=1, per-sector DRQ window re-armed by ide_sector_read_cb); status 0x50 after both

WRITE SECTORS (0x30) single @ scratch LBA 64 (M8.4b): status 0x58 immediately after 0x30 (DRQ-out, no BSY), write 256 words via outw (pattern 0xC000+j, distinct from native 0x4000+j), status 0x50 after commit; read-back LBA 64 returns 0xC0xx per-record — PROVES the write took effect (non-vacuous)

WRITE SECTORS (0x30) multi @ LBA 70 nsector=2 (M8.4b): status 0x58 immediate, write sector 0 (256 words), status 0x58 at the boundary (DRQ RE-ARMED — the load-bearing multi-sector-write record), write sector 1 (256 words), status 0x50 after both; read-back LBA 70-71 (512 words) returns the written 0xC000..0xC1FF per-record — GATE-PROVES the WRITE continuation FSM + the data of both sectors
EXECUTE DEVICE DIAGNOSTIC (0x90): error(0x1F1)=0x01 (device 0 passed), status=0x50, devhead=0xA0 (ATA_DEV_ALWAYS_ON)
deterministic compute (esi=cyls=2, edi=0x7F017F00 sector-127 words 0/1, ebp=0x7F00AA55 sector-0 boot-sig low half, ecx=diag-error=1), isa-debug-exit code 133
why_per_record_differential: pide sets nIEN FIRST so NO IRQ14 is ever delivered — every IDE interaction is a synchronous IN/OUT or a polled-status read — so qemu's gdbstub single-step (gen_trace.py --system) IS a valid per-record oracle (the SSTEP_NOIRQ HW-IRQ masking is structurally irrelevant: no IRQ is raised). The gate is plain compare.py EQUIVALENT (10854 records), running on ventium_soc with the M8.4 IDE device + the -drive image.
disk_value_match: gen_disk.py emits BOTH artifacts from ONE in-memory byte buffer: pide.img (qemu -drive if=ide,format=raw,index=0) and pide.disk.hex (ven_ide $readmemh). They cannot drift (a build-time drift assert reconstructs the buffer and compares). qemu's ide_data_readw is cpu_to_le16 (identity on the LE host) so a 16-bit data-port read = (buf[2j+1]<<8)|buf[2j]; the RTL returns {disk[2j+1],disk[2j]} for the same j -> byte-identical READ SECTORS by construction. Disk geometry 128 sectors -> qemu guess_chs_for_size = cyls 2/heads 16/secs 63 (ven_ide parameterized identically). NOTE: CYLS is an INDEPENDENT ven_ide parameter (not derived from DISK_SECTORS); the geometry coupling is a CONVENTION pinned to the committed 128/2/16/63 config (a code comment, not an enforced assert) — changing DISK_SECTORS without re-deriving CYLS/HEADS/SECS would diverge the IDENTIFY geometry words, which the gate would then catch.
identify_pinning: ven_ide builds the IDENTIFY 256-word block: the geometry words (w0/1/3/4/5/6/54/55/56/57/58/60/61/100) are COMPUTED from the DISK_SECTORS/CYLS/HEADS/SECS parameters; the model/serial/firmware strings (w10-46) and the feature/capability words (w20-22/47-53/59/62-69/80-88/93/106/169) are config-pinned constants captured from qemu 8.2.2 for the fixed single -drive if=ide,index=0 config (e.g. w85 WCE = 0x4021, the OBSERVED value, NOT a spec guess). These are documented build-pinned data (same category as the disk image); the per-record compare is REAL — the committed RTL constants are graded against a FRESHLY generated golden each run, so any qemu drift is caught.
oracle_boundaries: OFF the differential surface, documented: (1) the IRQ14 line-edge instruction boundary — the test sets nIEN so no IRQ is ever raised (ide_bus_set_irq guards on !DISABLE_IRQ); the line is wired to PIC IR14 for connectivity (quiescent, like the IR1/IR8/IR12 precedent). (2) the inter-instruction async-BSY-vs-synchronous timing — qemu fetches sector data via an async bottom-half that completes BEFORE the next single-step, so the golden never observes a lingering BSY between instructions; the RTL settles synchronously within the S_IO window, so a polled status read sees the same 0x58 -> the poll loop runs the same count on both sides (empirically verified: 10854 records match, same count). MODELING BOUNDARIES the adversarial review surfaced — all on paths the directed test provably does NOT reach (so the EQUIVALENT result is honest), documented here rather than hidden: (a) ABSENT SLAVE is a SINGLE-SHARED-REGISTER-FILE model — qemu masks ONLY error/status/alt-status to 0x00 for the selected absent slave (the test reads exactly + only those, matching on both sides), but qemu returns the slave's INDEPENDENT reset-signature shadow (lcyl/hcyl=0xFF) for 0x1F2-0x1F6 whereas the RTL returns the shared master value (0x00); the test never reads those slave registers. (b) DATA PORT read while DRQ=0 returns the stale buffer word in the RTL vs 0x0000 in qemu (core.c:2429); the test reads 0x1F0 only inside DRQ-gated drain loops. (c) the SECONDARY channel (0x170-0x177/0x376) is left UNDECODED in ventium_soc (reads 0x00) — note this is a REAL value divergence, not a quiescent no-op: qemu's default -machine pc auto-creates an empty ide1-cd0 (non-NULL blk) so its secondary status reads 0x50; the directed test touches only the primary (0x1F0-0x1F7/0x3F6), so it is unreachable. (d) the WRITE SECTORS path (M8.4b) adds two off-surface boundaries, both shared with READ's class: the async write-back BH (qemu's ide_sector_write sets BUSY 0xD0 then commits in a block-layer BH that settles before the next single-step, so the golden never shows a lingering BSY; the RTL commits synchronously within the S_IO window -> same poll-count parity), and the word-by-word disk[] commit (the RTL writes each word to disk[] on its own clock whereas qemu buffers the 256 words and commits the whole 512 bytes at end-of-sector — identical CPU-observable result since the written LBA is not re-read until a later READ); a stray data-port write while DRQ=0 is dropped by the RTL guard (r_status[3] && in_write), matching qemu (ide_data_writew DRQ/pio-out guard). The M8.4b WRITE adversarial review surfaced four further divergences, ALL on gate-unreachable paths (verified: the test writes only in-range LBAs 64/70/71, devhead 0xE0, full drain/fill before each command, outw only in write windows), documented here not hidden: (e) OUT-OF-RANGE LBA (>=128) — the RTL has NO range check and disk_byte uses only xfer_lba[6:0], so LBA>=128 ALIASES mod-128: a WRITE SILENTLY CORRUPTS the in-range sector and completes 0x50/0x00, whereas qemu range-checks (ide_sect_range_ok core.c:405) and cleanly ABORTS 0x41/0x04 with the disk untouched (same wrap affects READ + any multi-sector transfer crossing LBA 127->128). (f) LBA-REGISTER ADVANCE — qemu's ide_set_sector (core.c:638) updates sector/lcyl/hcyl/select to start+count after a completed transfer; the RTL leaves them at the issued values (the test never reads the task-file LBA regs post-transfer). (g) COMMAND-WHILE-DRQ/BSY — qemu (ide_bus_exec_cmd core.c:2155) ignores a non-RESET command mid-transfer; the RTL command arm has no such guard. (h) READ-DURING-WRITE-DRQ — a data-port READ during a write window advances+returns disk[] in the RTL (read arm guards only DRQ, not direction) vs qemu's 0x0000 (ide_data_readw core.c:2429). Deferred to a later ATA-misc-commands milestone: SET FEATURES/SET MULTIPLE and all other non-{EC,20,21,30,31,90} ATA commands (the RTL blanket-aborts them with 0x41/0x04 whereas qemu completes many, e.g. 0x91 INIT-DEV-PARAMS / 0x10 RECALIBRATE with 0x50/0x00 — value-verified out-of-band, NOT gate-exercised), the four divergences (e)-(h) above, CHS addressing, software reset (SRST, an async BH in qemu), the secondary channel, bus-master DMA.
comparison_plan: verif/soc/run-soc-ide-gate.sh: (0) gen_disk.py emits pide.img + pide.disk.hex + drift assert; (1) build pide.bin + the ventium_soc --soc TB with -DVEN_IDE_DISK_HEX; (2) confirm pide.bin reaches isa-debug-exit (133) under qemu-system-i386 WITH the -drive (drive MANDATORY: no-drive aborts IDENTIFY/READ); (3) regenerate the per-record golden via gen_trace.py --system --args -drive ... (records drift-checked vs the committed pide.sys.vtrace.golden); (4) run ventium_soc on pide.bin; (5) compare.py --mode func -> EQUIVALENT over all retired instructions.
description: Bare-metal M8.4a/b SoC IDE/ATA differential (NO paging, NO interrupts — nIEN-polled). Boots real->protected, then synchronously exercises the IDE/ATA controller M8.4 wires into ventium_soc — the primary channel command block (0x1F0-0x1F7) + control block (0x3F6), one drive (primary master, PIO mode): reset signature, absent-slave masking, IDENTIFY DEVICE, READ SECTORS (LBA 0 + 127, single + a 2-sector multi-sector read), WRITE SECTORS (M8.4b: single @ scratch LBA 64 + multi-sector @ LBA 70/71, each proved by a read-back of the written pattern), EXECUTE DEVICE DIAGNOSTIC. Every interaction is a synchronous IN/OUT or polled read, graded PER-RECORD against the qemu-system-i386 single-step golden (with a matching -drive). The disk is a single-source-of-truth image (gen_disk.py -> pide.img for qemu + pide.disk.hex for the RTL $readmemh), so READ SECTORS (and the WRITE read-back) data is byte-identical by construction; qemu writes go to a snapshot=on overlay so the committed pide.img stays pristine (an md5 PRE==POST gate assert enforces it). Proves the M8.4 IDE wiring byte-identical to qemu over all 10854 retired instructions; the IRQ14 cadence + async-BSY timing are documented oracle boundaries off the differential surface.
